signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Peace Trade
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4375 (implied) — NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags as oil prices collapse 19% toward $90 on reports of an imminent US-Iran peace deal. The 'peace trade' removes the Hormuz blockade risk premium, disproportionately weighing on CAD while supporting high-beta NZD. NZD/USD at 0.5963 eyes the 0.6000 handle as the dollar index (DXY) hits a 2.5-month low.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. Speculators remain heavily short despite a hawkish central bank stance and a +150bp USD/NZD carry advantage that is narrowing on US-Iran peace hopes. If RBNZ signals a terminal rate hike, short-squeeze risk targets 0.6050. NZD/USD at 0.5963.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7242 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 90% short. Institutional buyers remain in control as price tests the 2022 swing area resistance. The extreme retail short positioning provides contrarian fuel for a break higher if peace optimism persists. RBA at 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry tailwind for AUD.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Technicals
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.727 (z=+1.83σ) — the pair is decoupling from traditional equity-linked risk sentiment. EUR/USD at 1.1761 is extending a sentiment-led rally toward 1.1825 despite a -175bp carry disadvantage vs USD. The break suggests the move is driven by DXY weakness rather than Eurozone fundamental outperformance.
